title: Section — Bid Log
description: The bid-management log. The top block lists every trade with its estimated budget (pulled from the trade budget pages, which pull quantities from the QTO), a confidence score, and a variance range. The bottom block lists subcontractor bids: every bidder who was invited or intends to bid, a flag for who actually responded, and their actual bid amount. Listed in CSI MasterFormat division order. This layer logs and structures the bids; it does NOT level them, validate the low bid, or pick a winner — that is vendor-bid-leveling's job.

trades.description: One row per trade: the estimated budget and how confident we are in it.

estimated_budget.description: Pulled from the trade's budget page total.

confidence_score.description: How confident the estimate is (0..1), driven by QTO confidence and scope completeness.

variance_low.description: Low end of the expected cost range.

variance_high.description: High end of the expected cost range.

bids.description: Subcontractor bids: invited / intends-to-bid / responded, with actual amounts.

invited.description: Was this sub solicited / provided the bid docs?

intends_to_bid.description: Did they indicate they intend to respond?

responded.description: Have they actually submitted a bid?

bid_amount.description: Actual submitted amount (present only when responded).

exclusions.description: Verbatim; a blank is never assumed to mean 'included'.
